title: astragalus venulosus subsp. venulosus
intro: Astragalus venulosus subsp. venulosus, also known as the Alpine milk-vetch, is a plant species belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae) and is native to high-altitude mountainous regions. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Astragalus venulosus subsp. venulosus include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and potentially lowering blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are taken orally to harness its medicinal properties.
